page: Amazon Web Services r6i.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 64 physical cores and 128 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per physical core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the r6i.metal demonstrates top-tier multi-core processing capabilities, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Passmark CPU scores. Its cryptographic and extended instruction capabilities also rank in the top tier, aided by hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES. Memory performance is strong, with read-only large block bandwidth reaching 338.62 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 133.14 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25% of tested systems.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal configuration eliminates virtualization overhead, maximizing the utility of its massive 1 TB memory footprint and 128 vCPUs for resource-intensive workloads. The server is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput Redis deployments, enterprise database operations, and memory-bound data processing tasks. It also supports moderate LLM inference workloads, particularly for prompt processing where it achieves strong performance.
description: Amazon Web Services r6i.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server designed for high-capacity workloads. Powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors, it delivers 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) at 3.5 GHz, paired with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The server lacks local storage and GPUs but features a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmarks, the r6i.metal excels in multi-core processing and cryptography, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU tests. Its memory subsystem delivers strong read and write bandwidth, placing it in the top 25%. This hardware profile is highly efficient for memory-intensive applications, avoiding virtualization overhead. It is best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise database operations, Redis deployments, and heavy data processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6i.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6i.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs and 1024 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity bare-metal server featuring one terabyte of memory and top-tier multi-core performance for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz
1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per physical core
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and cryptographic execution capabilities
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPUs
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis, and enterprise workloads
